title: Service Outcomes, Node 24 LTS, and the TypeScript Toolchain · Type-System Trace: Typescript Native Compatibility
summary: A community-service API counts routes as value, floats Node and npm versions, confuses TypeScript type erasure with validation, and cannot reproduce its build or startup decision. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts typescript native compatibility increment in outcome, runtime, compiler, and reproducibility charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate the TypeScript 7 native CLI from the TypeScript 6 compatibility API and prove which tool owns checking, emission, editor APIs, and build gates.
Pin Node 24.18 LTS, npm 12, architecture, package policy, lockfile, environment, and supported deployment assumptions as one reproducible runtime contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Typescript Native Compatibility · Theory Typescript Native Compatib…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 5014 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a reproducible Node and TypeScript artifact instead of trusting local residue; record only bounded redacted diagnostic fields; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate the TypeScript 7 native CLI from the TypeScript 6 compatibility API and prove which tool owns checking, emission, editor APIs, and build gates.
